method.name: ToMap
method.comment:
Converts a JDBC ResultSet to a Map, with the value of the first column used as the key, and a Map of the columns used as the value. The column types of the columns are used to determine the types of the keys and values to be added to the Map (e.g. Integer for Types.INTEGER, etc.). If the column types are not recognized, the values are added as Strings.
Example output: {"John": {"Name": "John", "Age": 30}, "Jane": {"Name": "Jane", "Age": 25}} rs: The ResultSet to convert. Return type: @return:A Map containing the values from the ResultSet.

method.name: ToDataSeries
method.comment:
Converts a JDBC ResultSet into a Map containing two ArrayLists: one for series values and another for data values. The first column's values are added to the series ArrayList, while the second column's values are added to the data ArrayList.
Example output: { "series": [series_value1, series_value2, ...], "data": [data_value1, data_value2, ...] } rs: The ResultSet to convert. Return type: @return:A Map with keys "series" and "data", containing ArrayLists of the respective values.

method.name: ToMap_KVP
method.comment:
Converts a JDBC ResultSet to a Map, with the first column's values used as the keys, and the second column's values used as the values. The column types of the columns are used to determine the types of the keys and values to be added to the Map (e.g. Integer for Types.INTEGER, etc.). If the column types are not recognized, the values are added as Strings.
Example output: {"John": 30, "Jane": 25} rs: The ResultSet to convert. Return type: @return:A Map containing the values from the ResultSet.

method.name: ToMap_IVP
method.comment:
Converts a JDBC ResultSet to a Map, with the row number as the key and the value of the first column as the value. The column type of the first column is used to determine the type of the value to be added to the Map (e.g. Integer for Types.INTEGER, etc.). If the column type is not recognized, the value is added as a String.
Example output: {1: "John", 2: "Jane"} rs: The ResultSet to convert. Return type: @return:A Map containing the values from the ResultSet.

method.name: ToListOfMaps
method.comment:
Converts a JDBC ResultSet to an ArrayList of Maps, with the column names used as the keys in the Map. The column type is used to determine the type of the value to be added to the Map (e.g. Integer for Types.INTEGER, etc.). If the column type is not recognized, the value is added as a String.
Example output: [{"Name": "John", "Age": 30}, {"Name": "Jane", "Age": 25}] rs: The ResultSet to convert. Return type: @return:An ArrayList containing the values from the ResultSet.

method.name: GetFieldTypes
method.comment:
Returns a Map containing the column names from the ResultSet as the keys, and the corresponding JDBC type name as the value.
Example output: {"Name": "VARCHAR", "Age": "INTEGER"} rs: The ResultSet to convert. Return type: @return:A Map containing the column names and their corresponding JDBC type names.

method.name: GetFieldList
method.comment:
Returns a List containing the column names from the ResultSet.
Example output: ["Name", "Age"] rs: The ResultSet to convert. Return type: @return:A List containing the column names.

method.name: ToList
method.comment:
Converts a JDBC ResultSet to an ArrayList, with the first column's values used as the elements in the ArrayList. The column type of the first column is used to determine the type of the value to be added to the ArrayList (e.g. Integer for Types.INTEGER, etc.). If the column type is not recognized, the value is added as a String.
Example output: ["John", "Jane"] rs: The ResultSet to convert. Return type: @return:An ArrayList containing the values from the ResultSet.

method.name: ToListOfLists
method.comment:
Converts a JDBC ResultSet to an ArrayList of ArrayLists, with the first ArrayList containing the column names (if includeHeader is true), and subsequent ArrayLists containing the values from each row. The column type is used to determine the type of the value to be added to the ArrayList (e.g. Integer for Types.INTEGER, etc.). If the column type is not recognized, the value is added as a String.
Example output: [["Name", "Age"], ["John", 30], ["Jane", 25]] rs: The ResultSet to convert. includeHeader: If true, the first ArrayList will contain the column names. Return type: @return:An ArrayList containing the values from the ResultSet.
